title_en: The Best LEGO Architecture Sets Worth Your Time and Money - IGN
summary_en: From the Statue of Liberty to the Eiffel Tower, these are our picks for the bets LEGO Architecture sets available now.
body_en:
LEGO's Architecture line takes us on a globetrotting tour – from a Wonder of the Ancient World to the skyline of a modern city. Is creating a structure from real life more difficult than creating something new, out of whole cloth? The latter has the benefit of a first impression – there are no expectations of what should be , as opposed to what is.
But with LEGO Architecture, there are abstract concerns. Is a LEGO designer trying to create an exact replica of the subject? Or are they creating something that, while it may not capture the exact measurements of the thing, captures the spirit of it – the image we have in our minds when we hear its name.
Below we've detailed the best LEGO Architecture builds that you can purchase as of June 2026. If you still need more LEGO when you're done, check out our roundup of the very best LEGO sets or our picks of the best sets for adults .
One of the Architecture sets with the highest piece counts, this recreation of Neuschwanstein Castle comes with foliage that you can change to reflect the seasons. Commissioned by Ludwig II and completed in 1886, the original castle is famous for its beautiful Bavarian setting, and it served as the inspiration for Sleeping Beauty's castle in Disneyland.
One of the most popular locations in Rome, the Trevi Fountain has a coin throwing tradition; toss one in, and it means you'll return to the city. This model includes three minifigures, representing the iconic statues of Oceanus, Abundance, and Health.
The actual Notre Dame de Paris has undergone constant building, rebuilding, and redesign since its first stone was laid in 1163. You build this LEGO model in the same chronological order as the original, starting with the curved rear of the building and ending with the spire. You get a history lesson to go along with the impressive end result.
A gift to America from France, Lady Liberty welcomes immigrants to New York's shores. She's an American icon, and this set has all her key details – even the shackle and chain at her feet, symbolizing new freedom.
Unlike many of the other builds on this list – which show these landmarks as they exist today – the LEGO Great Pyramid of Giza takes us back to 26th century B.C., when the pyramids still had their white limestone covering. This set also comes with two smaller pyramids, a sphinx, and a facsimile of the Nile River. You can open the pyramid to reveal the passageways and royal chambers inside.
This set captures the architecture of Himeji Castle, a 700-year-old fort and one of the most popular landmarks in Japan.You can open the roof to reveal the interiors, and the decorative elements include the country's pink cherry trees.
The LEGO Eiffel Tower is, as of June 2025, the set with the highest piece count in LEGO history – over 10,000 pieces, which combine to form one of the most iconic structures in the world. The tower includes three observation decks and a set of elevators, and it stands close to five feet tall. It's currently one of the most expensive LEGO sets you can buy, and it's definitely a monument to the monument itself.
Similar to the brick-built 'postcards that LEGO likes to sell, this build is a 3D depiction of Paris, including landmarks such as the Eiffel Tower, Notre-Dame de Paris Cathedral, the Arc de Triomphe , and the Louvre. When complete, it makes an excellent display for your wall or work desk.
LEGO's latest rendition of the New York City is visually interesting, depicting the sky as a massive apple silhouette covered in stars. It also Includes numerous New .York landmarks including the Brooklyn Bridge, the Empire State Building, the Guggenheim Museum, the Metropolitan Museum of Art, the Statue of Liberty, One World Trade Center, adthen and the Statue of Liberty,
According to the official LEGO Store's handy filter , there are currently 10 LEGO Architecture sets available for purchase as of June 2026. But oddly, the LEGO Eiffel Tower is classified as an 'Icon' rather than as an Architecture build. It might be a good idea to browse the rest of the site to ensure that there aren't other, similar builds that slip through the filter's cracks.
LEGO Architecture sets are some of the best LEGO sets for adults , because by design they're not really made in any way to appeal to average kids.
In a way, it's surprising that there aren't more of these sets. The Museum of Modern Art, for example, partners with LEGO and sells The Starry Night in their gift shop, and when I went there with my family a few months ago, they were completely sold out. Surely, most architectural tourist destinations could benefit from a replica set – an ultimate souvenir – for visitors to take home and remember their travels by.
For more, check out our picks for the best Nintendo LEGO sets , as well as the best LEGO Ninjago sets and best cheap LEGO sets .
